text:
FOUNDATIONONE CDx COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

==========================================================
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
==========================================================
Patient Name: Margaret Choi
Date of Birth: [REDACTED]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: [Treating Oncologist, Gastrointestinal Medical Oncology]
Institution: [Academic Medical Center, Multidisciplinary Pancreatic Oncology Program]
Specimen ID: [FNDTN-XXXXXX]
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core biopsy tissue
Procedure Type: Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS)-guided fine needle biopsy (FNB)
Anatomic Site: Pancreatic head mass
Date of Procedure: [see clinical record]
Date Specimen Received at Foundation Medicine: [see clinical record]
Report Date: [see clinical record]
Test Ordered: FoundationOne CDx (FDA-approved comprehensive tumor profiling; 324-gene panel)
Indication: Newly diagnosed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, borderline resectable; evaluation for targetable genomic alterations and clinical trial eligibility

==========================================================
QUALITY METRICS
==========================================================
Tumor Purity (estimated): 40-45% (adequate for analysis)
Mean Exon Coverage Depth: 718x
Pathologic Diagnosis Confirmed on Submitted Specimen: Moderately differentiated ductal adenocarcinoma of the pancreas
Specimen Adequacy: Adequate

==========================================================
SUMMARY OF GENOMIC FINDINGS
==========================================================

CLINICALLY RELEVANT ALTERATIONS (Tier I / Tier II):
- KRAS p.G12V (c.35G>T) - Activating point mutation; variant allele frequency (VAF) 42%
- TP53 p.R248W (c.742C>T) - Loss-of-function / dominant-negative point mutation; VAF 38%
- CDKN2A - Homozygous deletion (copy number = 0; complete biallelic loss)
- SMAD4 p.R361H (c.1082G>A) - Loss-of-function point mutation; VAF 35%
- ARID1A p.Q1178* (c.3532C>T) - Nonsense (stop-gain) mutation; VAF 18%
- STK11 p.L67fs*35 (c.198_199delCT) - Frameshift mutation resulting in premature stop codon; VAF 20%

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
- MYC amplification: 8 copies (high-level amplification; chromosome 8q24.21)
- GATA6 amplification: 7 copies (high-level amplification; chromosome 18q11.2)

NEGATIVE FINDINGS (relevant to therapeutic decision-making):
- BRCA1: No pathogenic mutation detected
- BRCA2: No pathogenic mutation detected
- PALB2: No pathogenic mutation detected
- ATM: No pathogenic mutation detected
- NTRK1: No fusion detected
- NTRK2: No fusion detected
- NTRK3: No fusion detected
- BRAF: No mutation detected (V600E and non-V600E)
- ERBB2 (HER2): Not amplified; no activating mutation detected
- ALK: No rearrangement detected
- ROS1: No rearrangement detected
- RET: No rearrangement detected
- FGFR1/2/3/4: No amplification or fusion detected
- NRG1: No fusion detected
- KRASG12C: Not present (KRAS mutation is G12V, not G12C)

MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) STATUS:
- MSI Status: MSI-Stable (MSS)
- Method: Fragment-length analysis of 95 loci

TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB):
- TMB: 4 mutations/Mb (LOW)
- Threshold for TMB-High: >=10 mutations/Mb

HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION DEFICIENCY (HRD):
- HRD Signature: Not detected
- Genomic Loss of Heterozygosity (gLOH): 8% (below threshold of 16% used for HRD designation)
- No BRCA1/2 or other HRR pathway gene pathogenic mutations detected

MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) STATUS (by IHC, performed at submitting institution):
- MLH1: Retained (positive)
- MSH2: Retained (positive)
- MSH6: Retained (positive)
- PMS2: Retained (positive)
- Overall MMR Status: Proficient MMR (pMMR)

==========================================================
DETAILED VARIANT ANNOTATIONS
==========================================================

1. KRAS p.G12V (Glycine-to-Valine substitution at codon 12)
   - Classification: Pathogenic activating oncogenic mutation
   - VAF: 42%
   - Clinical Significance: KRAS G12V is the second most common KRAS codon 12 mutation in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), present in approximately 10-12% of PDAC cases (KRAS G12D being most common at ~40%). KRAS G12V results in constitutive activation of the RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK and PI3K-AKT-mTOR signaling axes, driving tumor proliferation and survival. As of the time of this report, no KRAS G12V-specific inhibitors with regulatory approval for pancreatic cancer exist; this allele is not amenable to sotorasib or adagrasib (which are selective for KRAS G12C). No FDA-approved targeted therapy is currently available for KRAS-mutant PDAC. Pan-KRAS inhibitors and KRAS G12D inhibitors (e.g., MRTX1133) are in early clinical development. Clinical trial enrollment is encouraged.
   - Co-mutation Context: The concurrent presence of TP53 mutation and CDKN2A deletion in addition to KRAS mutation represents the "classical" PDAC mutational tetrad (with SMAD4 also altered), associated with aggressive biology.

2. TP53 p.R248W (Arginine-to-Tryptophan substitution at codon 248)
   - Classification: Pathogenic loss-of-function / dominant-negative mutation
   - VAF: 38%
   - Clinical Significance: R248W is one of the most common TP53 hotspot mutations across all cancers and among the most frequent in PDAC. This variant abolishes the DNA-binding capacity of p53 and also exerts a dominant-negative effect over wild-type p53. Loss of p53-mediated cell cycle arrest, apoptosis, and DNA damage response contributes to genomic instability and chemotherapy resistance. No direct targeted therapeutic is currently clinically available against TP53-mutant PDAC.

3. CDKN2A Homozygous Deletion
   - Classification: Pathogenic tumor suppressor loss (complete biallelic inactivation)
   - Copy Number: 0 (homozygous deletion)
   - Clinical Significance: CDKN2A encodes p16INK4a (and p14ARF from the same locus), a critical negative regulator of CDK4/6-mediated cell cycle progression through the G1/S checkpoint. Homozygous deletion of CDKN2A is present in approximately 30-40% of PDAC and constitutes one of the four canonical genetic alterations driving pancreatic carcinogenesis. Loss of p16 leads to unchecked CDK4/6 activity, unrestrained Rb phosphorylation, and unrestricted S-phase entry. CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) have shown limited single-agent activity in PDAC but remain under investigation in combination regimens, particularly in CDKN2A-deleted tumors. Germline CDKN2A deletions/mutations are associated with familial atypical multiple mole melanoma (FAMMM) syndrome and increased PDAC risk; germline testing is recommended and has been offered to this patient.

4. SMAD4 p.R361H (Arginine-to-Histidine substitution at codon 361)
   - Classification: Pathogenic loss-of-function mutation
   - VAF: 35%
   - Clinical Significance: SMAD4 (also known as DPC4 - Deleted in Pancreatic Cancer locus 4) encodes a central transcriptional mediator of the TGF-beta signaling pathway. SMAD4 mutations or homozygous deletions are present in approximately 50-55% of PDAC cases and are associated with a more aggressive clinical phenotype, including increased propensity for widespread metastatic dissemination (particularly hepatic and peritoneal). SMAD4 loss has been proposed as a predictive biomarker of poor response to definitive surgery (associated with occult micrometastatic disease) and is a negative prognostic marker in resected PDAC. IHC-based SMAD4 (DPC4) protein expression loss was also confirmed on the diagnostic biopsy specimen at the submitting institution. This finding is consistent with the sequencing result. SMAD4 restoration or TGF-beta pathway targeting in PDAC remains investigational.

5. ARID1A p.Q1178* (Glutamine-to-Stop codon at position 1178)
   - Classification: Pathogenic loss-of-function nonsense mutation
   - VAF: 18%
   - Clinical Significance: ARID1A encodes a subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex. Nonsense and frameshift mutations in ARID1A cause loss of protein expression and impaired chromatin remodeling, affecting transcriptional regulation of numerous target genes. ARID1A mutations occur in approximately 8-12% of PDAC. While ARID1A-mutant tumors have been associated with sensitivity to EZH2 inhibitors and PARP inhibitors in some tumor types (synthetic lethality), these associations have not been validated in PDAC. The relatively lower VAF of 18% compared to the primary driver mutations (KRAS 42%, TP53 38%, SMAD4 35%) is consistent with subclonal acquisition. No approved ARID1A-targeted therapy exists for PDAC.

6. STK11 p.L67fs*35 (Leucine-67 frameshift, premature stop at codon 101)
   - Classification: Pathogenic loss-of-function frameshift mutation
   - VAF: 20%
   - Clinical Significance: STK11 (also known as LKB1) is a serine-threonine kinase and tumor suppressor that regulates cellular energy sensing through the AMPK-mTOR pathway. STK11 mutations are most commonly associated with Peutz-Jeghers syndrome (germline) and non-small cell lung cancer (somatic). In PDAC, STK11 alterations are uncommon (approximately 3-5% of cases) and may reflect a distinct molecular subtype. STK11 loss has been associated with an immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment and resistance to immune checkpoint inhibitors in lung cancer; its implications in PDAC are under investigation. Notably, STK11-mutant tumors with co-occurring KRAS mutation may have upregulated mTORC1 signaling. The VAF of 20% suggests possible subclonal presence.

7. MYC Amplification (8 copies)
   - Classification: Pathogenic oncogenic copy number amplification
   - Clinical Significance: MYC is a master transcriptional regulator driving cell cycle progression, metabolic reprogramming, and ribosome biogenesis. MYC amplification (located at 8q24.21) occurs in approximately 5-10% of PDAC and is associated with aggressive tumor behavior, increased proliferative index, and poor prognosis. High-level MYC amplification (as seen here with 8 copies) has been proposed as a predictor of poor prognosis in resected PDAC. Direct MYC inhibition remains challenging; indirect approaches (BET bromodomain inhibitors, CDK7/9 inhibitors) are in clinical development.

8. GATA6 Amplification (7 copies)
   - Classification: Copy number amplification; uncertain direct therapeutic implication
   - Clinical Significance: GATA6, located at chromosome 18q11.2, encodes a zinc finger transcription factor critical for pancreatic progenitor cell differentiation. GATA6 amplification has been identified as a molecular marker of the "classical" PDAC molecular subtype (as defined by Collisson, Bailey, and TCGA classification systems), which is associated with a more epithelial phenotype, better differentiation, and potentially improved response to chemotherapy compared to the "basal-like" (quasi-mesenchymal) subtype. The concurrent presence of GATA6 amplification with SMAD4 loss, CDKN2A deletion, and KRAS/TP53 mutations is consistent with the classical PDAC subtype with high-risk genomic features.

==========================================================
THERAPEUTIC IMPLICATIONS SUMMARY
==========================================================

FDA-APPROVED THERAPIES WITH DIRECT BIOMARKER MATCH (On-Label):
- NONE identified for this tumor profile at the current time
  * KRAS G12V: No approved KRAS G12V inhibitor for pancreatic cancer
  * pMMR/MSS: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) not indicated (requires dMMR/MSI-H; tumor is pMMR/MSS)
  * TMB-Low (4 mut/Mb): Does not meet TMB-High threshold (>=10 mut/Mb) for pembrolizumab
  * No NTRK fusion: Larotrectinib (Vitrakvi), entrectinib (Rozlytrek) not applicable
  * No BRCA1/2 pathogenic mutation: Olaparib (Lynparza) maintenance not applicable; rucaparib not applicable
  * No HRD signature detected
  * HER2/ERBB2 not amplified or mutated
  * No BRAF V600E: Vemurafenib, dabrafenib/trametinib not applicable
  * No RET/ROS1/ALK rearrangement

POTENTIAL INVESTIGATIONAL OPPORTUNITIES / CLINICAL TRIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- KRAS-mutant PDAC: Enrollment in trials targeting KRAS (pan-RAS/pan-KRAS inhibitors, KRAS G12D inhibitors, downstream MEK/ERK inhibitors in combination)
- CDKN2A-deleted PDAC: CDK4/6 inhibitor trials in CDKN2A-null pancreatic cancer
- SMAD4-null PDAC: SMAD4 status-stratified PDAC trials; trials evaluating TGF-beta pathway inhibition
- MYC-amplified PDAC: BET bromodomain inhibitor or CDK7/9 inhibitor trials
- STK11-mutant: mTOR pathway inhibitor combination trials
- Tumor microenvironment-directed immunotherapy combinations (despite pMMR/MSS status): stroma-targeting, CAR-T, cancer vaccines targeting KRAS neoantigens

GERMLINE TESTING RECOMMENDATION:
- Germline genetic testing is strongly recommended given:
  (a) CDKN2A homozygous somatic deletion (germline CDKN2A pathogenic variants associated with FAMMM syndrome and markedly elevated PDAC risk; also elevated risk in first-degree relatives)
  (b) Patient age and clinical context (pancreatic adenocarcinoma at age 58 without family history of PDAC does not exclude hereditary predisposition)
  (c) NCCN guidelines recommend germline testing in all PDAC patients
- Patient was counseled regarding germline testing recommendation; patient declined germline testing at this time. Recommendation documented. Revisitation at future clinical encounter encouraged.

==========================================================
METHODOLOGY NOTE
==========================================================
FoundationOne CDx is an FDA-approved in vitro diagnostic (IVD) next-generation sequencing (NGS) test that uses hybrid capture-based enrichment of 324 cancer-relevant genes plus select intronic regions for detection of structural rearrangements. The assay detects substitutions, insertion-deletions (indels), copy number alterations, and selected gene fusions/rearrangements from FFPE tumor tissue. MSI status is assessed across 95 microsatellite loci. TMB is calculated from the somatic coding mutation rate (substitutions and indels) across the sequenced genome space, normalized to mutations per megabase, and reported as low (<10 mut/Mb), intermediate (10-19 mut/Mb), or high (>=20 mut/Mb). HRD assessment is performed by genomic loss of heterozygosity (gLOH) analysis. Variants of unknown significance (VUS) are not reported in this summary; full variant data including VUS is available in the detailed data appendix upon request. This report was generated in a CLIA-certified, CAP-accredited laboratory.

==========================================================
INTERPRETING PHYSICIAN ATTESTATION
==========================================================
This genomic profiling report has been reviewed and interpreted by a board-certified molecular pathologist. The findings described above are based on analysis of the submitted tumor specimen and reflect the mutational landscape of the sampled tumor at the time of biopsy. Clonal heterogeneity may limit representation of all tumor subclones. Results should be interpreted in the clinical context by the treating physician in consultation with molecular tumor board or genetic counselor as appropriate.

Interpreting Pathologist: [Name and Credentials, Foundation Medicine]
Medical Director Signature: [On File]

==========================================================
END OF REPORT
==========================================================
